name: Atemporal Body-Track Glitch Timeline
description: Reusable style template for a bugged camera timeline: start with clean source footage, jump into short green motion-glitch events, return to normal between filtered moments, then switch into a red body-track look made from small points, edge lines and trails instead of large circles. The recipe builds the performable TOP network and documents the edit grammar in timeline_notes; the artist still arranges exact source-time jumps or render/export timing after import. For true MediaPipe landmarks, run setup_body_tracking and layer pose_skeleton_mediapipe or body_tracking_reactive over the red branch.

nodes.name: source
nodes.comment: Drop the source video here. For live use, swap to videodeviceinTOP and keep the same downstream wiring.

nodes.name: clean_pass
nodes.comment: Unfiltered source branch. Use this for the opening and recovery moments.

nodes.name: green_edges
nodes.comment: Motion/contour extraction before the green glitch shader.

nodes.name: green_glitch
nodes.comment: Green horizontal tear shader. Fast, synthetic, camera-bug energy.

nodes.name: green_feedback
nodes.comment: Previous green frame for trail accumulation.

nodes.name: green_mix
nodes.comment: Adds current green tracker over its decayed trail.

nodes.name: green_decay
nodes.comment: Green trail decay. Higher values leave longer trails.

nodes.name: red_edges
nodes.comment: Contour extraction feeding the red tracker shader.

nodes.name: red_tracker
nodes.comment: Red tracking style: small points, edge lines and traces. No large circles.

nodes.name: red_feedback
nodes.comment: Previous red tracker frame for red point/line trails.

nodes.name: red_mix
nodes.comment: Adds current red tracker over its decayed trail.

nodes.name: red_decay
nodes.comment: Red trail decay. Keep this high enough that motion points leave a visible trace.

nodes.name: mode_switch
nodes.comment: 0 = clean, 1 = green glitch, 2 = red body-track. Keyframe this index for the final timeline.

nodes.name: timeline_clock
nodes.comment: 15s edit clock matching the reference export length. Use it to keyframe mode_switch.index.

nodes.name: timeline_notes
nodes.comment: Readable edit grammar for this look.

parameters.name: green_feedback_target
parameters.description: feedbackTOP samples the decayed green composite to build trails.

parameters.name: red_feedback_target
parameters.description: feedbackTOP samples the decayed red composite to build point/line trails.

glsl_uniforms.name: uTear
glsl_uniforms.label: Green Tear
glsl_uniforms.description: Horizontal band displacement amount for the green glitch branch.

glsl_uniforms.name: uThreshold
glsl_uniforms.label: Red Threshold
glsl_uniforms.description: Brightness threshold for emitting small red tracking points.

glsl_uniforms.name: uPointScale
glsl_uniforms.label: Red Point Scale
glsl_uniforms.description: Size of the small red tracking points. Keep modest to avoid the large-circle look.

python_code.timeline_notes:
Atemporal body-track glitch edit grammar

0.0-2.2s clean original footage, original audio
2.2-3.4s green glitch, atemporal source jump
3.4-4.2s clean recovery
4.2-5.5s red body-track branch, small red points/lines/trails, no large circles
5.5-7.2s clean original footage
7.2-8.5s green glitch, different source-time offset
8.5-10.5s clean recovery
10.5-11.8s red body-track branch, not directly after green
11.8-12.8s clean snapback
12.8-13.7s green glitch
13.7-14.9s green glitch again, final bug hold

Perform by keyframing mode_switch.index: 0 clean, 1 green, 2 red. Keep filtered sections short, around 1-2 seconds. Add glitch/noise audio only while mode_switch is 1 or 2. Verify final exports at real 30 fps, not slow-motion preview frames.

preview_description: A 9:16 source-video template with clean footage, fast green camera-glitch tears and a red body-track branch made from small points, edge lines and feedback trails. Keyframe SceneMode for the final bug timeline; the included notes preserve the exact timing grammar from the reference edit.
